concept_records.canonical_key: chain of thought prompting chain of thought prompting w e0b808cfbb48

payload.core_idea: Prompting the language model to generate intermediate reasoning steps before producing the final answer.
payload.description: Prompting baseline that elicits intermediate reasoning steps before the final answer.
payload.discussion: Chain-of-Thought Prompting is informative only when it is reported under the same benchmark protocol, metric, and resource budget as the evaluated method. The comparison tests whether the source work's gains come from the proposed mechanism rather than from the established baseline mechanism: Expose a serial reasoning trace and measure whether explicit steps improve accuracy at an added token cost.
payload.evidence: Represents an improved baseline that still suffers from unfaithful reasoning despite generating intermediate steps, against which the 18.4% improvement is measured.
payload.methodology: Uses prompts such as 'Let's think step by step' or provides few-shot examples of step-by-step reasoning to guide the model's output, attempting to mimic human reasoning processes.

performance.value_text: 18.4% lower than the proposed neuro-symbolic framework.

payload.principle: Expose a serial reasoning trace and measure whether explicit steps improve accuracy at an added token cost.
payload.source_paper_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
payload.source_work_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning

concept_records.canonical_key: error driven self refinement loop error driven self refinement loop if a symbolic solver rejects a formalization due to syntax or consistency errors then the specific error message must be fed back to the language model to iteratively revise the symbolic representation until a valid execution w e0b8

payload.abstract_signature: If a symbolic solver rejects a formalization due to syntax or consistency errors, then the specific error message must be fed back to the language model to iteratively revise the symbolic representation until a valid execution is achieved.
payload.argument: If a symbolic solver rejects a formalization due to syntax or consistency errors, then the specific error message must be fed back to the language model to iteratively revise the symbolic representation until a valid execution is achieved.
payload.canonical_key: if symbolic solver rejects formalization due to syntax or consistency errors then specific error message must be fed back to language model to iteratively revise symbolic represent
payload.discussion: This mechanism treats the symbolic solver not just as an inference engine but as a rigorous validator that provides actionable feedback. By appending solver error messages to the prompt, the language model is guided to correct specific logical or syntactic flaws in its translation, effectively learning from its immediate failures within the inference context. This iterative process mitigates the risk of the pipeline failing due to minor formatting issues or initial misinterpretations of complex constraints.
payload.evidence: The abstract and introduction explicitly describe a 'self-refinement module' that utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. This loop continues to correct the output of the problem formulation stage.
payload.name: Error-Driven Self-Refinement Loop
payload.problem_pressure: I would like to design a framework that lets LLMs extract logical patterns from other exemplars during inference and use the logical patterns to improve the reasoning accuracy-token frontier.
payload.source_paper_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
payload.source_work_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning

concept_records.canonical_key: semantic to symbolic translation with deterministic verification semantic to symbolic translation with deterministic verification if a reasoning task requires guaranteed logical faithfulness then the probabilistic generation of reasoning steps must be replaced by a deterministic symbolic inference e

payload.abstract_signature: If a reasoning task requires guaranteed logical faithfulness, then the probabilistic generation of reasoning steps must be replaced by a deterministic symbolic inference engine, preceded by a semantic-to-symbolic translation layer handled by a language model.
payload.argument: If a reasoning task requires guaranteed logical faithfulness, then the probabilistic generation of reasoning steps must be replaced by a deterministic symbolic inference engine, preceded by a semantic-to-symbolic translation layer handled by a language model.
payload.canonical_key: if reasoning task requires guaranteed logical faithfulness then probabilistic generation of reasoning steps must be replaced by deterministic symbolic inference engine preceded by
payload.discussion: The principle establishes a strict division of labor where the language model acts solely as a parser for ambiguous natural language, while the actual logical deduction is offloaded to a system that adheres to formal logic rules. This architecture ensures that any error in the final conclusion stems from either a translation error or a lack of knowledge in the premises, rather than a hallucinated inference step. The deterministic nature of the solver provides a verifiable chain of logic that probabilistic models cannot inherently offer.
payload.evidence: The text states that while LLMs can imitate reasoning, they lack a mechanism to guarantee faithfulness. In contrast, symbolic inference engines are faithful because they follow well-defined inference rules. The proposed framework addresses this by having an LLM convert natural language into symbolic formulation, which a deterministic solver then processes.
payload.name: Semantic-to-Symbolic Translation with Deterministic Verification
payload.problem_pressure: I would like to design a framework that lets LLMs extract logical patterns from other exemplars during inference and use the logical patterns to improve the reasoning accuracy-token frontier.
payload.source_paper_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
payload.source_work_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning

payload.canonical_key: standard chain of thought prompting still suffers from unfaithful reasoning where conclusions do not follow generated steps whereas proposed method guarantees faithfulness through
payload.condition: Necessary when the application demands that every step of the reasoning process be logically valid and verifiable, rather than just statistically likely.
payload.discussion: The work highlights that while Chain-of-Thought improves performance by encouraging step-by-step generation, it does not solve the fundamental issue of unfaithful reasoning where the model's conclusion contradicts its own stated premises. The proposed framework addresses this by ensuring that the reasoning steps executed by the symbolic solver are mathematically guaranteed to follow from the premises. This distinction is crucial for domains where explainability and logical rigor are non-negotiable.
payload.evidence: The introduction notes that recent studies found LLMs occasionally make unfaithful reasoning even with chain-of-thought, as they lack a mechanism to guarantee faithfulness, unlike symbolic inference engines which follow well-defined rules.
payload.finding: Standard Chain-of-Thought prompting still suffers from unfaithful reasoning where conclusions do not follow generated steps, whereas the proposed method guarantees faithfulness through deterministic solving.
payload.main_results: Standard Chain-of-Thought prompting still suffers from unfaithful reasoning where conclusions do not follow generated steps, whereas the proposed method guarantees faithfulness through deterministic solving.
payload.message_text: Standard Chain-of-Thought prompting still suffers from unfaithful reasoning where conclusions do not follow generated steps, whereas the proposed method guarantees faithfulness through deterministic solving.

concept_records.canonical_key: neuro symbolic decomposition with self refinement neuro symbolic decomposition with self refinement logical reasoning accuracy is enhanced by separating natural language translation from logical inference assigning the former to a large language model and the latter to a deterministic symbolic solve

payload.canonical_key: logical reasoning accuracy is enhanced by separating natural language translation from logical inference assigning former to large language model and latter to deterministic symbol
payload.core_idea: Logical reasoning accuracy is enhanced by separating natural language translation from logical inference, assigning the former to a large language model and the latter to a deterministic symbolic solver, while employing a feedback loop that uses solver error messages to iteratively correct symbolic formalizations.
payload.discussion: The mechanism mitigates the risk of hallucinated reasoning steps common in probabilistic models by enforcing a separation of concerns. The language model leverages its strength in semantic understanding to parse ambiguous natural language, while the symbolic solver guarantees the mathematical validity of the inference. The self-refinement module is essential for handling the initial imperfections in the model's translation, effectively using the solver as a rigorous validator. However, the efficacy of this method depends on the ability to map the problem domain into a formal logic system that the solver can process.
payload.evidence: The text describes a framework that decomposes logical reasoning into problem formulation, symbolic reasoning, and result interpretation. It explicitly mentions a self-refinement module that utilizes symbolic solver error messages to revise formalizations, contrasting this with the black-box nature of standard chain-of-thought prompting which lacks mechanisms to guarantee faithfulness.
payload.idea_text: Logical reasoning accuracy is enhanced by separating natural language translation from logical inference, assigning the former to a large language model and the latter to a deterministic symbolic solver, while employing a feedback loop that uses solver error messages to iteratively correct symbolic formalizations.
payload.mechanism: The process begins with the language model converting a natural language problem into a symbolic formulation containing entities, facts, and rules. This formulation is passed to an external deterministic symbolic solver. Upon successful execution, the result is interpreted and mapped to an answer. If the solver fails due to syntax or inconsistency, it generates an error message. This message is appended to the original prompt, instructing the language model to revise the symbolic formulation. This loop continues until the solver produces a valid result or a maximum attempt limit is reached.

evidence_records.claim_text: The text describes a framework that decomposes logical reasoning into problem formulation, symbolic reasoning, and result interpretation. It explicitly mentions a self-refinement module that utilizes symbolic solver error messages to revise formalizations, contrasting this with the black-box nature of standard chain-of-thought prompting which lacks mechanisms to guarantee faithfulness.
evidence_records.snippet: The text describes a framework that decomposes logical reasoning into problem formulation, symbolic reasoning, and result interpretation. It explicitly mentions a self-refinement module that utilizes symbolic solver error messages to revise formalizations, contrasting this with the black-box nature of standard chain-of-thought prompting which lacks mechanisms to guarantee faithfulness.

evidence_records.claim_text: The text states that while LLMs can imitate reasoning, they lack a mechanism to guarantee faithfulness. In contrast, symbolic inference engines are faithful because they follow well-defined inference rules. The proposed framework addresses this by having an LLM convert natural language into symbolic formulation, which a deterministic solver then processes.
evidence_records.snippet: The text states that while LLMs can imitate reasoning, they lack a mechanism to guarantee faithfulness. In contrast, symbolic inference engines are faithful because they follow well-defined inference rules. The proposed framework addresses this by having an LLM convert natural language into symbolic formulation, which a deterministic solver then processes.

evidence_records.claim_text: The abstract and introduction explicitly describe a 'self-refinement module' that utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. This loop continues to correct the output of the problem formulation stage.
evidence_records.snippet: The abstract and introduction explicitly describe a 'self-refinement module' that utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. This loop continues to correct the output of the problem formulation stage.

upload_decisions.title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning

identity.canonical_title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
identity.title_norm: logic lm empowering large language models with symbolic solvers for faithful logical reasoning

work_version_records.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown human-like reasoning abilities but still struggle with complex logical problems. This paper introduces a novel framework, Logic-LM, which integrates LLMs with symbolic solvers to improve logical problem-solving. Our method first utilizes LLMs to translate a natural language problem into a symbolic formulation. Afterward, a deterministic symbolic solver performs inference on the formulated problem. We also introduce a self-refinement module, which utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. We demonstrate Logic-LM's effectiveness on five logical reasoning datasets: ProofWriter, PrOntoQA, FOLIO, LogicalDeduction, and AR-LSAT. On average, Logic-LM achieves a significant performance boost of 39.2% over using LLM alone with standard prompting and 18.4% over LLM with chain-of-thought prompting. Our findings suggest that Logic-LM, by combining LLMs with symbolic logic, offers a promising avenue for faithful logical reasoning. Code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/teacherpeterpan/Logic-LLM.

metadata.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown human-like reasoning abilities but still struggle with complex logical problems. This paper introduces a novel framework, Logic-LM, which integrates LLMs with symbolic solvers to improve logical problem-solving. Our method first utilizes LLMs to translate a natural language problem into a symbolic formulation. Afterward, a deterministic symbolic solver performs inference on the formulated problem. We also introduce a self-refinement module, which utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. We demonstrate Logic-LM's effectiveness on five logical reasoning datasets: ProofWriter, PrOntoQA, FOLIO, LogicalDeduction, and AR-LSAT. On average, Logic-LM achieves a significant performance boost of 39.2% over using LLM alone with standard prompting and 18.4% over LLM with chain-of-thought prompting. Our findings suggest that Logic-LM, by combining LLMs with symbolic logic, offers a promising avenue for faithful logical reasoning. Code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/teacherpeterpan/Logic-LLM.

payload.title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning

payload.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown human-like reasoning abilities but still struggle with complex logical problems. This paper introduces a novel framework, Logic-LM, which integrates LLMs with symbolic solvers to improve logical problem-solving. Our method first utilizes LLMs to translate a natural language problem into a symbolic formulation. Afterward, a deterministic symbolic solver performs inference on the formulated problem. We also introduce a self-refinement module, which utilizes the symbolic solver's error messages to revise symbolic formalizations. We demonstrate Logic-LM's effectiveness on five logical reasoning datasets: ProofWriter, PrOntoQA, FOLIO, LogicalDeduction, and AR-LSAT. On average, Logic-LM achieves a significant performance boost of 39.2% over using LLM alone with standard prompting and 18.4% over LLM with chain-of-thought prompting. Our findings suggest that Logic-LM, by combining LLMs with symbolic logic, offers a promising avenue for faithful logical reasoning. Code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/teacherpeterpan/Logic-LLM.
